reviews.review_text:
[h1] GREAT STORY, TERRIBLE REPETITIVE GRIND [/h1]

[b][i]  66 hours spent in New Bordeaux. To be completely honest, Mafia 3 is a game of missed potential. The story of Lincoln Clay, the cinematic documentary-style storytelling, and the legendary 60s soundtrack are absolutely incredible. The combat feels heavy and brutal.

However, the mission design is an absolute nightmare. The game forces you into an endless, exhausting loop of "take over rackets, interrogate captains, and repeat" just to progress the main story. It ruins the pacing of what could have been a masterpiece.

It's a decent revenge story, but you need a lot of patience to survive the repetitive grind.

The family isn't who you're born with, it's who you die for. 7/10  [/i][/b]

[h1]Mafia III: Definitive Edition – amazing story trapped inside repetitive gameplay[/h1]

[b]⭐ Rating: 7/10

[i]Lincoln Clay is an absolute tank. Play the story and you’ll understand.[/i]

[hr]

[h2]Story & Characters[/h2]
Mafia III genuinely surprised me with how strong the story is. Lincoln Clay, a Vietnam veteran returning home hoping for a normal life, quickly became one of my favorite protagonists in gaming.

The story has real emotional weight and depth. It also doesn’t shy away from showing the struggles and racism Black people faced during that era, which adds a lot of authenticity and impact to the world.

This actually feels like what becoming a crime boss should feel like.

[hr]

[h2]World, Atmosphere & Design[/h2]
The open world is fantastic.

The cars, city design, music, lighting, environments, and overall atmosphere create one of the most authentic Mafia experiences I've played. The motion capture and cinematics are surprisingly good, and animations, traversal, and gunplay all feel smooth and satisfying.

As a game design student, I really appreciated the environmental design and how well the world captures the vibe of that period.

[hr]

[h2]DLCs – Massive W[/h2]
The DLCs honestly saved the repetitive gameplay loop for me.

[b]Faster, Baby![/b] → Pure chaos, gave me strong Just Cause energy.

[b]Stones Unturned[/b] → Easily my favorite. Straight-up Uncharted vibes and a completely different feel.

[b]Sign of the Times[/b] → Felt like Resident Evil randomly entered Mafia and somehow it actually worked.

Huge respect to the devs for trying different ideas instead of repeating the same formula.

[hr]

[h2]The Problems[/h2]
The biggest issue is [b]repetition.[/b]

You repeatedly do the same racket control activities just to reach the next target, and after a while it becomes really annoying.

Technical problems were also frustrating:

• Game struggles with very high FPS
• At 1440p Max settings (150–170 FPS), .1% lows were terrible
• Gameplay felt choppy and almost unplayable
• Had to cap to 60 FPS + enable V-Sync and Motion Blur for smoothness
• No Borderless Windowed mode (pain for dual-monitor users)

Also the enemy AI sometimes randomly turns into John Wick and deletes you in one second.

[hr]

[h2]Final Thoughts[/h2]
Mafia III is a weird one.

Amazing story ✔️
Great characters ✔️
Authentic Mafia atmosphere ✔️
Fantastic DLCs ✔️

Repetitive gameplay ❌
Technical issues ❌

Lincoln Clay alone carried this game hard for me.

[b]7/10 – flawed but absolutely worth playing for the story and one of gaming’s most underrated protagonists.

I went into Mafia III expecting a decent crime game.

What I didn't expect was spending hours cruising around New Bordeaux with the radio on, completely absorbed in a city that feels equal parts beautiful and broken.

Lincoln Clay isn't your typical gangster protagonist. He's angry, determined, and impossible not to root for. The story doesn't glorify crime as much as it tells a revenge tale with consequences, and that's what kept me playing even when the mission structure started repeating itself.

Is the gameplay repetitive? Absolutely.

Did I still take the long route to every objective just to hear one more song on the radio? Also absolutely.

Mafia III is one of those games that's easy to criticize on paper but surprisingly hard to put down once you're invested in its world. It's rough around the edges, but it has more personality than many technically better open-world games.

Not perfect. Not for everyone.

But years after release, Lincoln Clay's story is still one I'll remember.
I actually like mafia 3 i just wanted to replay it but this definitive edition is complete buns buggy mess hangar 13 should be ashamed of themselves

The good things: Story is interesting, gameplay is solid, nice music selection.
However, this is called "definitive" edition and it is soooo buggy. I can laugh about visual bugs but there are soft locks, glitchy achievements and other things that just annoy you. 
Also the game design is boring. Before you can do the interesting story stuff you have to endure taking over some random turf which is really repetetive.
Also super minor thing that only I care about but the menu is one of the worst things ever with how it slides weirdly from one tab to another lol

I tried to give this game a chance because I thought it would get better. Unfortunately, it didn't, it remained a repetitive slog.

My main complaint with this game is that it's incredibly boring. Everything you do consists of going from point A to point B, killing or interrogating somebody, and then returning to point A, only to be given yet another objective that's exactly the same.

In addition, the main character simply has no interesting personality. He's out for revenge, and the entire narrative revolves around an "I eat my enemies for breakfast" attitude. It's boring and not engaging in the slightest.

What a shame that a follow-up to such story-driven, linear games as Mafia and Mafia II turned out this way.
Do you love bugs? Especially the kind of bugs that makes the game never start after uninstalling and reinstalling? Then, you will probably like this buggy mess

Mafia III definitely is not a perfect game and has rough edges being first game from Hanger 13 as well as bugs that are present even after 10 years from release.

And yet, even with different basic gameplay formula it holds great as a sequel to Mafia franchise. Forget anything you know about it a give it a chance. You might find that the "bad game" from the past might surpass quite of a few contemporary AAA-titles. 

Gameplay might feel repetitive at time but splitting game session helped me a lot. The character and the story is great, serious and mature. 
And meeting some of the familiar faces was both a pleasant surprise as well as a natural evolution for the plot.

It is definitely worth a try,
the gameplay is repetitive and boring
The game looks great, but the gameplay isnt.
i tried to like it but i cant they just have a bunch of busy work for a single player game and it just there to make the game longer
just terrible. mechanics, story, cannot change the key mapping. one of the worst games I have played in years

If you want a Mafia game in the shape of a GTA franchise with a bit of repetetive missions than this is a game for you. Most probably the weakest of the series, however with an interesting story to tell. If you dont have anything smarter to play, go for it and maybe you will like it.
Story telling: 9/10
Gameplay: 6/10
Graphics and sound: 7/10

I've played through this game twice already on xbox and I find myself wanting to play it again. The story is great, the need for revenge, the need and want to ruin those who have wronged the MC. The payback story doesn't hide itself, it throws you right into it not only with the story but with how you play too. 

Like with another game, max payne, you can't hold too much ammo and it at times forces you to be aggressive or sneaky. I played sneaky last two times because of the lack of ammo and the game can feel dry and lacking but when you mix it up with closing the distence by bolting towards them if they spot you and start beating them up like you in a movie THEN start shooting, it's actually really fun. There's also a slo mo mechanic to the shooting that you can activate. I use it more for dramatical effect while closing the distance more than trying to hit my shot. I'm already a good shot. Aim assist will shaft you though, it's a straight up handicap so I'd suggest turning it off. 

The driving is fun too, I like drifting around corners in clays muscle car. You can try the simulated driving mode which only makes the cars functionality more life like but turning becomes a pain so I switched back to normal. There's also a slo mo mechanic for driving too which I again would only use for dramatical effect because I'm the best at driving.

There are some graphical glitches with the skybox, it's always been in the game ever since release and it's hard not to miss when the game is night time. The clouds would jitter, most likely due to the time lapse at night. Don't really notice it in the day. It's a bit jank but for me doesn't ruin the game. 

What does ruin the game is when it crashes. In 3 hours of play time it's already crashed. Not really sure why but I was moving a game from one hard drive to another at the time so it was probably on me. 

They also got the black gang repping purple and the fleur de lis like they be the 3rd st saints from the OG saints row games. Problem is, they are insistent on selling heroin where clay grew up and it doesn't sit well with me how their leader speaks to poor clay, always trying to manipulate him into doing stuff for her because it's for his fellow black people. If she cared, she wouldn't sell heroin to her people. She will get dealt with in time. Vito and the Irish are better allies, they don't care that clay is black yet in a time where racism is a real problem, those gents treat our boy with respect, so I will give them all the territory out of respect to their colourblindness instead of cassandra who doesn't shut up about it. But before I deal with the "not saints" I will do all of their missions and gain all the unlocks THEN I will shaft them because I'm smart like that, also, it's heretical to have your gang colour purple and have the gang logo be the fleur de lis and not call yourself the 3rd st saints, filthy posers, for that alone, they will be ended for their transgressions
Not the best Mafia but definitely the better one, buy only if on sale.
Good gunplay, driving as well, but the missions can be notably repetitive.

I've tried to play this game four separate times now and I just can't do it. It is such a slog. You're almost immediately thrown into a bland open world that's effectively just a giant checklist of generic locations to go clear out, for some reason Vito is in the game (don't get excited, he's just here to nostalgia bait and assign more generic missions), and the gunplay is somehow worse than the previous games. My brain almost refuses to accept that this game sucks because of how great the first two games are, so I keep trying to come back to this every year or two, but I just can't do it anymore. Tragedy

This game has an amazing and unique setting, you are playing as a black man in racist America. The story is amazing, as to be expected from mafia games. Gameplay is also very solid and holds up well.

However there is a lot of grind, I highly recommend downloading Less Grind mod on nexus mods as it fixes the main issues this game has and makes the experience very enjoyable.

i beat this game way back and remember people would ♥♥♥♥ on it.

Apparently, the game's too repetitive. Personally, the amazing soundtrack helps me get through it all without feeling so. 

It's an amazing game that gets a bad rep. Arguably better than TOC

Story? Alright so far. Interesting setting that's for sure.

Gameplay? A little boring and repetitive. It's a couple of fetch/kill mechanics on infinite repeat with lots of pointless collecting.

The other big issue is technical. This game has so many damn bugs. Missions will randomly reset their objectives and undo hours of work. They fail to complete. Objectives fail to register or even appear on the map.

I can only recommend it if you've enjoyed the first two games. I wouldn't recommend jumping into the series at this point.

The game is good, but it doesnt stick to the "Mafia games formula", its closer to mad max and fallout 4 in terms of gameplay rather than a Mafia game. The story was great and i think Joe is the driver at the end of the game but overall i would give it a 7.5/10

It has nice setting and music, but you can't save a game based on that.  
Plot premise is mostly revenge story and incredibly predictable. 
Main character is boring and soulles, there are no character development whatsoever, you're playing for a mindless brute from start to finish. 
Gameplay is INCREDIBLY repetative. The developers of this game do not respect your time which you'll going to be wasting going from one part of the city to another doing the same boring go kill/fetch quests over and over again.  The whole middle game is just mindless grind which never pays out in amy meaningful way. 

If you really want to finish it to "beat all mafia games" just do prologue, capture a district for Vito  and then watch last mission and his ending on Youtube. Whatever is in the middle is not worth your time and have literally ZERO affect on the ending. 

I adore original Mafia (remake is great too), Mafia 2 have special place in my heart but this? This is not a mafia game at all. I feel sorry for the time wasted on this.
Would have been a descent game if not for the horrible amount of bugs. Nothing game-breaking though.

Mafia 3 is basically a punisher game but the story and music are Goated, Only downfall would be it's repetitive gameplay ,but still i highly recommend this game. (you will feel goosebumps when you hear paint it black for the first time)
It's a fun open city game but it doesn't have the soul of Mafia and Mafia II if that's what you're looking for. Feels more like Saints Row than GTA tbh.

I could see how the gameplay would feel repetitive to a lot of people but i hate rednecks so i never stopped having fun. The NPCs are dumb af though.

Youll walk into an area where 3 bad guys are standing around a civilian npc. The npc has a bag over his head and hes covered in blood. Hes begging for his life in a scripted dialogue. Youll think "Oh i gotta save this guy quickly" and shoot the bad guys. The npc will enter default panic mode and run past you in a totally normal voice "Run for the hills!" or smth like "Gah damn hes got a gun!". All of the panic and emotion instantly vanishes when combat starts. No "Thanks for saving me!" or "I was almost a goner!". Just the same 10 lines of dialogue for every civ npc on the street. 

The game is fun, the shooting is fun, it rarely glitches, the voice acting is great, and the callbacks arent forced. The gameplay and everything attached to this game is fun if not just a bit repetitive. The civ npcs are stupid and all the same.
The game can be a bit repetitive near the end but other than that I really enjoyed Mafia 3

This game is great BUT terribly optimized . It only runs great with three vanilla/base game options:
1.) Volumetric Lighting = Low
2.) Reflection Quality = Low
3.) FPS = 60 fps
These three settings turned any higher were cooking my graphics card, and it seems from what I've read online, other people were having the same issues with those settings . I have been having a lot of fun with this installation of the Mafia series, though personally I'd consider it to be a step away from how the previous two played out . The combat is smooth and fun, I love the races, and I love how Mafia 3 welcomes and offers different playstyles on how the player wants to handle a job . I wouldn't say you'd need to play the first two in the series to enjoy this as a standalone, though I always recommend the entire Mafia series .

Loved the game. Returned to it after it didn't catch me the first time on the PS4. I'm a huge fan of the series. After it's release it was bombed by the press and fans alike. I think that's why I just wanted to not like it when I first played it. Now after so many years I really had fun with the game. Yes it's sometimes repetive but I loved the setting, story, music and especially the DLC's. There so much more to like about this game then to dislike.
Solid game. But repetitive the dlcs and actual story line is really good.

Did not expect this game to be THIS long. I beat it at around 24+ hours and still feel like there’s a ton of stuff in it. Multiple endings too, like 5 or something. The DLCs were also surprisingly fun.

I couldn’t relate to Lincoln Clay as much as Tommy Angelo from Mafia 1 though. Tommy was just a regular guy getting dragged into the mafia life, while Lincoln is basically a Vietnam veteran Terminator that can destroy entire buildings by himself. Still a cool protagonist, just a very different vibe.

The gameplay did get repetitive sometimes with constantly taking over districts, flipping informants, driving across the map, taking down rackets, repeat. The driving itself is actually really good though, which helps because 90% of the game is driving somewhere.

Main villain was solid too. I liked that he felt human instead of cartoonishly evil. Also really cool seeing Vito Scaletta again (mafia 2 main character)

Not sure I’d replay it, but I can definitely see why some people love this game.

The mouse setting is completely F-A-K UP.

The RefreshRate when doing FullScreen is locked at 60, with no MenuOption given.

The ArtWork is decent. Sadly I unable to explore it with a broken mouse setup of the game.

This game was a really pleasant surprise. I bought it a few years ago, and at the time I couldn’t even boot it, so I threw it in the “trash”. A few days ago I had nothing to play, so I installed it again to see if it would work this time (I have a new system now).

It’s not nearly as bad as people made it out to be. The story is great, the city is atmospheric and set in an interesting environment, the characters and their writing are memorable, and the MC became one of my favorites. The soundtrack is amazing, and the gameplay is fun, but it’s dragged down by the quest design. Conquering all districts becomes tedious — there are 9 districts, each with 2 rackets doing the same type of missions, and only the capo missions have a more cinematic approach. The DLCs are amazing: more story‑driven and they add some cool mechanics or gadgets to the main game while also breaking the grind of the main story.

I had some technical issues — one crash, and a few quests got bugged to the point where I had to restart from the last checkpoint. Fortunately, the game autosaves often.

If you can tolerate or push through a few hours of mindless grind, you’ll enjoy this game.
Story didn't hold up in the end game mechanics were pretty much the same as previous not alot of change.
Great game, can be repetitive most of the time, but the story is phenomenal

I’ve played around 36 hours of Mafia III and finished it 3 times, and honestly I still think it’s one of the most underrated Mafia games. The atmosphere is amazing, the story is strong, and the setting feels unique compared to most open-world games.

The game takes place in New Bordeaux, inspired by 1960s New Orleans, and the world has a lot of personality. The music is probably one of the best parts of the game — driving around while listening to classic songs makes the experience feel cinematic. Lincoln Clay is also a great protagonist. His revenge story feels brutal, emotional, and personal from start to finish.

The gunplay is satisfying, especially during intense fights and stealth sections. Taking over districts and building your criminal empire feels rewarding at first, and the cutscenes are extremely well made. The documentary-style storytelling gives the game its own identity and makes it stand out from other crime games.

But the game definitely has problems too. After a while, missions become repetitive because you keep doing similar objectives over and over: destroy businesses, interrogate people, and eliminate bosses. Even though the gameplay loop can still be fun, it starts to feel grindy in the middle of the story. The AI is also not the smartest sometimes, and there are a few technical issues and bugs here and there.

Still, despite the repetition, I kept coming back to the game because the atmosphere, soundtrack, characters, and story are just that good. Few games make you feel like you’re inside a classic crime movie the way Mafia III does.

Overall, I’d give Mafia III an 8.5/10.
It’s not perfect, but if you enjoy story-driven open-world games with a dark revenge story and incredible music, it’s definitely worth playing.

Somehow time went quick and it became a classic. I did had fun in it. PS I advice to set Vsync in game ON too, otherwise there is a bug flying car at random bumps, is because new graphic cards and screens reach more frames and game engine thinks something else.
I do not understand why people hate on this game. I understand that it is a bit grindy but the story and the character performances more than make up for it. Would recommend it to everyone.

great story, Lincoln clay as a character and the beginning of this game is just phenomonal. However, after gaining all 3 of the bosses the missions and controlling districts become incredibly repetitive. I was toughing out those last few hours oif the main repetitive missions however just to finish the story. worst mafia game out of the 3, 6/10.

The only "open world" thing about it is that you can choose what mission to choose first. 
But for the rest it's an amazing game just like the other Mafia games.

Nice game, but the saves don't save - at least for me with the most recent update.  This is a bug others have too.  There are no manual saves of course, but the auto save feature is broken.  If you check on Reddit others have this problem as well.  

I have various steam achievements showing how far I have progressed, but after quitting the game and reloading, I'm suddenly back at the start of the first major mission and all progress is lost.  This has happened to me 2x and its a waste of time.  

A request for support was responded to with an email showing that no one had read my message.  Avoid.

Honestly one of the best games i've played in a while but it is a bit glitchy. when your driving the game lags and some of the buildings turn black. also sometimes the voices of the characters don't match up. Other then that it has really good game play, And the revenge aspect adds a bit of intriguement to the game. The missions are somewhat the same with the abjectives but nothing that bugs me at least. i'd give this game a soild 8/10. with a patch fix it would be a solid 9.8

The story is good. You will have multiple choices during the game and access to different endings. The soundtrack is great if you enjoy old hits.

Graphics are good, but not state of the art. Driving is nice, but better AI would help with better driving experience. The culture and social issues of that time are well epresented in the game.

On the down side, the game feels grindy. The upgrades are very expensive. The weapons are very expensive. The wiretapping takes significant time. The tasks to make money feel monotonous.

I feel like after taking one district over, and having an underboss, you should be allowed to delegate some boring tasks. Instead, the opposite happens, these characters ask you to personally go steal some cars or trucks, like a low level mobster.

There are multiple bugs that force you to restart the game. They don't happen often, but I had one happen right after a cutscene, which makes me wonder how that bug is allowed to go through in the final release.

Overall, I recommend to buy it on sale.

Defintely not the best in the series and extremely repetetive missions and bugs persist to this day. But its still... Mafia. The overall story and the start of teh story and especially the DLC mission (even though pretty disconnected except the weed one) were extremely good. If it wasnt for good mechanics in the game I would have left this game a while ago. But its still good and gets a lot of unwarranted hate that I feel it doesn't deserve.

Although I feel instead of focusing on more open world aspects it should have done the narrative thing that the successors of this game did would have made this game's story something it would have not been ashamed by.

A masterpiece of brutal revenge and 60s atmosphere that perfectly blends CIA espionage with empire-building, proving that loyalty is the only currency that matters in New Bordeaux. I recommend it to everyone.

There are a lot of negative things that have been said about Mafia 3, so instead, I'll speak of its positives. The story, is very nice, you shake hands with a lot of shady folks in the name of revenge, folks who'll more than likely stab you at the first sign of weakness. But that's just how it is, you need to leverage and work with other folks if you want to have a shot at getting back at the people who wronged you. The characters, the way the game portrays them is so authentic to the era, there are some folks that'll share your woes with the white man's america, while there is the literal KKK. In fact, I had to keep cool when an officer called me a slur, because then I'd be in for a gunfight that I didn't want lol. The gameplay, is as repetitive as you may have heard, but the gunplay is responsive, satisfying, and brutal, especially at the highest difficulty (Which I recommend. The enemies hit you harder, but they still go down relatively quickly, 3 shots to the body, and of course, 1 to the head) 

Overall, if you're looking for a game that portrays the 1960's accurately, flaws and all, then you've found the game for you.

I think this is a disappointment in the Mafia lineup, especially given its potential.
Mafia 3 had a unique concept, great acting performances, a great soundtrack, and pretty solid gameplay.

But it's also completely overwhelmed with bugs, glitches, game design, and pacing issues. In its base, unmodded version, the game has glaring issues. The game frequently crashes and softlocks, or just has game-breaking glitches. The graphics and lighting are butchered with effects that are horribly optimized. The game's settings are lacking for a modern AAA game. When it IS working, the gameplay, while solid, satisfying, and full of style, also becomes incredibly repetitive and shallow. The DLCs and story save the grindy nature of it if you're patient.

I will say, however, if you decide to mod the game, the experience is faaaar better (thanks to the community patch from Nexus). It reduced about 70 percent of crashes and glitches and seriously corrected some lighting issues. I still had to really fine-tune the game's settings on my GPU to get it to run without stuttering, and then I could finally see the game's potential, unperturbed.

TL;DR:
A HARD No for the game in its current state. It's a game with potential that had awful execution and is totally divorced in quality from the rest of the series.

A SOFT yes if you're patient enough to mod and fine-tune optimization to actually play the game, and if you're open to a unique setting and characters that serve as an experimental departure from the Mafia franchise
Alles fängt schon damit an, dass man in dem Game ja nicht mal vom Wasser aufs Land gehen kann. Hahaha. Unfassbar.

damn this game is stinky

thing came out back when ham-fisting stealth into your games was all the rage still

as a result every shootout in this game feels like a failed stealth encounter and the game is trying to caudle the player to help them get stealth back on track. every damn time you try to have a fun loud shootout the enemy AI is in this constant state of being stupid and unaggressive. It absolutely sucks the fun out of this game's second to second enjoyment. This is only worsened by the fact that the story and vibe of the game are so heavily built for playing the game loud and aggressive.
This game is unplayable. From the color correction and physics to the storyline. The worst game in the franchise

One of the best AAA games I played so far, highly recommended online and they're not wrong. Amazing graphics for the time it was released, storyline is fantastic, plenty and varied missions, lots of guns and cars to choose from. Just amazing.

The story, graphics and voice acting is amazing, the gameplay however is not, well atleast not the missions. It's very repetitive, aka go to this place and destroy this and that until you have done enough damage and so on through out the whole game. 

So why did i recommend it? Because i was able to somewhat ignore it. The story got me so hooked that i wanted to keep going to see what was the end and i am happy i did that. I recommend this game if you're someone who can over look a flawed gameplay design for the sake of the voice acting, story and the graphics. If not then it's best to not play this game or atleast wait until it's on sale. 

I won't go into the story alot because i want people to experience it for themselves but.. i can say that every move i did. It really felt good and that i made a difference and an impact. I mean who doesn't want to play as a black veteran in a corrupted late 1960s where you take revenge from those in power. The story felt satisfying as hell. One of my favorites tbh and Lincon Clay gotta be one of my favorite protagonists.

TL;DR: If you're willing to overlook the flawed gameplay then play it for the story, graphics and voice acting. It's one of my favorite Mafia games in my opinion.

The game literally doesn't work 1. it cant even launch in fullscreen and when you load in theres ZERO options to changing the screen and the sensitivity you cannot change this what so ever on controller it stays at a almost unplayable sensitivity you can put it on the lowest and it wont matter horrible port im just glad i got it on sale awhile back.
rivaluto il gioco, l'inizio non mi è piaciuto molto, ma la storia è fatta molto bene peccato che alcune missioni siano molto ripetitive
starts off really good nice premise than right after the whole beginning prolouge bit it dives off a cliff and rest of it sucks

*Story: 8.5*
Well written characters and Licoln Clay it's a great protagonist. Aso, what I love about him it's the fact that he makes sense. Do to the fact that he was a soldier, it makes perfect sense for him to be able to use guns efficiently, to make those kind of takedowns and to have sharp mind. However, I think he has an absurd plot-armor (my dude got shot in the chest by a shotgun and got a bit knocked out). Also, he could've used some more character development. Yeah, do to the DLCs he got some character development, but if you exclude those, it's not really much left. He's kinda the same from the beggining to the end, more or less.

Also, the characters are great, the story it's also great and just a bit underrated and he could use a bit more memorable moments.

*Gameplay: 6/10*
Personally I've liked it, however it could've got some improvments/variaties. You see, I've got stuck using the same pistol (a silencer one) for almost the entire game, got to use only 2-3 guns and almost the same car since it was so great. Not that bad, but not great either.
The missions were so repetitive. I get it, it's not that simple to take down an organization. The gameplay reflects that quite well, but it's a video game so it doesn't work so well in this context.

Also, I've found New Bordeaux quite empty after finishing the game. Quite bad since I want

*Ambience: 9/10*
I really feel like I'm in 1968. The city, the music, the cars, everything makes it feel like you're in that era. I would've give him a 10 if the gameplay had activities to add to this vibe. The closest thing we had was the first mission who had a festival and the Southern Union.

*Graphics: 9/10*
Idk why this game got a remastered. It looked great as it was and, from what I've seen, the DE doesn't change much.

*Bugs: Less than I expected*
I've heard this game was filled with bugs, but I didn't encountered a game breaking one until the end of the game. Perhaps, I dind't encountered ANY bugs until the second half of the game. Also, most of them where my mini-map got zoomed in after leaving an interior, for example. Most of them were visual (when I was with my underbosses at the table, they where at 90° and under the floor).

*Conclusion: 8.7/10*
This could be one of the greates story in gaming. Or could've been. The game it's still enjoyable and I recommand to buy it with the entire definitive edtion series since, when they have discounnts, they are VERY low on price.
Slow moving boring game, weakest in the mafia series
This game sucks. Even if you're trying to like it there's basically nothing here to enjoy.

I want Reparations but I'll settle out of court for a case of Kool-Aid Pineapples.
CHUD BEWARE!!!
favorite mafia game even though people act like this game is a byproduct of the devil
Uninspired story, repetitive gameplay

One of my favorite games - ever. Sadly the "Definitive Edition" totally ruined the experience. When I bought the game I had a 24 inch 1920x1080 screen. I loved everything about it and played it through several times. 

Then came the "Definitive edition" and I bought the upgrade. This time I had a 5120x1440 screen. The game totally lost touch with the graphics. The map no longer works, action icons are no longer correctly connected to doors and other objects you can interact with. After many round with user support they just gave up.

The story was enjoyable and rich with some nice side stories and tasks. I really enjoyed the driving physics and the selection of cars. 

This is my absolute favorite candidate for a Remastered version!! Fix the graphics and add some cool featurers like an expansion of the garage. Not necessarily new cars, but the ability to add any available car to the selection. Would also love to see a couple of new side stories. 🙏

No matter what people say, this part of the Mafia series isn't a bad game. Personally, it gives me strong vibes of The Godfather games. If you look at Mafia 3 as the "Godfather 3" of the franchise, it's all about taking over businesses, but lacks the true spirit of a mafia family. Plus, the Rolling Stones on the soundtrack is a huge win! :3

I cannot recommend this game. Even with a Ryzen 7 7800X3D and an RTX 5080, it runs AWFUL. Even though my PC can technically push high framerates, the game engine just can't handle it-it suffers from constant stuttering and terrible frame pacing (or something like that probably). The worst part? Even on HIGH settings, it just doesn't look good. Textures are blurry, and the draw distance is terrible-everything renders maybe 50 meters in front of you, resulting in horrible pop-in for cars, people, and objects. Sometimes the crowd just disappears randomly, and the animations are trash; honestly, I haven't seen animations this bad in a long time. And that's just the technical side of things.

The story isn't any better. I've played for 3 hours, and the plot is just a massive cliché. To make matters worse, it's constantly interrupted by random, unnecessary cutscenes that are boring as hell. Three hours in, and I haven't done anything fun, epic, or remotely engaging enough to make me want to keep playing.

Overall, I absolutely cannot recommend this. If you are still thinking about buying the game, try the demo first. 1/10

great story, alot of dull minded side missions to do in between the amazing story telling narrative main missions mafia is known for. lack of depth in the open world aspect but a real cultivation main story of Lincoln clay.  DLC content brought it back to  more linear narrative based misions which is what mafia is widely known for. DLCs are worth picking up. also playing  over 60 fps i kept experiencing standing dead state npcs and glitches with falling npcs freezing in mid air. 6.5/10

Coming from Mafia 1 and 2, you might not like how Mafia III handles its story since it’s not really linear. But honestly, I still enjoyed it a lot.

Honestly though, the story telling carried the game for me. The way it’s presented with the documentary style stuff and flashbacks made it feel really unique. Absolute cinema at times haha.

The DLCs are also pretty solid and add more to the experience.
